titleEn: Fieldwork Robotics secures new funding to accelerate raspberry harvesting robot
titleZh: Fieldwork Robotics获新融资加速树莓采摘机器人商业化
summaryZh: 英国Fieldwork Robotics获得SEED Innovations等投资方共250万英镑种子轮融资，用于加速其自主树莓采摘机器人的商业化部署。该机器人已在英国商业农场测试，计划2027年实现多机器人集群商业运营。技术旨在解决劳动力短缺导致的果实损失问题，降低采摘成本并提升产量。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了采摘机器人在高价值水果领域的商业化进展，对智慧畜牧行业人士理解自动化技术在种植业中的落地路径和投资趋势有参考价值。
contentFull: Fieldwork Robotics secures new funding to accelerate raspberry harvesting robot 25-06 | Smart farming | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintFieldwork Robotics is currently testing its autonomous raspberry harvesting robot on commercial farms in the UK. The company expects fleets of multiple machines to enter commercial operation from 2027. Photo: Fieldwork Robotics.UK-based Fieldwork Robotics has secured additional investment from SEED Innovations, the investment company chaired by entrepreneur Jim Mellon, to accelerate the commercial deployment of its autonomous raspberry harvesting robot.The investment forms part of a £2.5 million (€2.9 million / $3.4 million) Seed+ funding round. It follows a previous £2.2 million (€2.5 million / $3.0 million) investment round completed in April 2026 and is complemented by £1.7 million (€2.0 million / $2.3 million) in grants from Innovate UK and a Climate Grant.Transition from technology validation to commercial farm deploymentFieldwork Robotics, a spin-out from the University of Plymouth, develops selective and modular robots for harvesting fresh raspberries. The company is currently deploying production machines through a 2-year Innovate UK ADOPT programme with Place UK in Norfolk and Littywood Farm in Staffordshire. According to CEO David Fulton, the additional funding will support the transition from technology validation to commercial farm deployment. The company plans to expand beyond the UK with trials in Australia and expects fleets of multiple robots to be operating commercially from 2027.Reducing harvesting costsSEED Innovations’ chairman Jim Mellon said the technology addresses one of the biggest challenges facing berry growers. He noted that labour shortages can lead to significant fruit losses and that autonomous harvesting offers a scalable solution to improve productivity. Fieldwork says it aims to deliver fruit quality comparable to that achieved by human pickers while reducing harvesting costs and enabling growers to scale production.LinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint Geert Hekkert Chief editor of Future FarmingMore aboutautonomous technologyharvesting technologyinvestmentroboticsadLEARN MORERelated articles02-07 | NewsTersus GNSS AG993: Modular autosteer kit with built-in satellite correction fallbackPremium30-06 | Photo reportDLG Feldtage: hot, dusty and relatively quiet25-06 | NewsAgreenculture acquires Chouette to add agronomic intelligence to autonomous machinesPremium24-06 | ArticleAustralian vegetable farm puts 2 weeding technologies to the test Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
总部位于英国的Fieldwork Robotics已从SEED Innovations获得额外投资，以加速其自主树莓采摘机器人的商业部署。SEED Innovations是由企业家Jim Mellon担任主席的投资公司。

该投资是250万英镑（约290万欧元/340万美元）种子+轮融资的一部分。此前该公司已于2026年4月完成220万英镑（约250万欧元/300万美元）的融资轮，并获得了Innovate UK和一项气候拨款共计170万英镑（约200万欧元/230万美元）的资助。

**从技术验证转向商业农场部署**

Fieldwork Robotics是普利茅斯大学的衍生公司，开发用于采摘新鲜树莓的选择性模块化机器人。该公司目前正通过一项为期两年的Innovate UK ADOPT项目，在诺福克郡的Place UK和斯塔福德郡的Littywood Farm部署生产型机器。据首席执行官David Fulton称，这笔额外资金将支持从技术验证向商业农场部署的过渡。该公司计划在澳大利亚开展试验，将业务拓展到英国以外，并预计从2027年起实现多机器人集群的商业化运营。

**降低采摘成本**

SEED Innovations主席Jim Mellon表示，该技术解决了浆果种植者面临的最大挑战之一。他指出，劳动力短缺可能导致大量水果损失，而自主采摘提供了一种可扩展的解决方案来提高生产力。Fieldwork表示，其目标是实现与人工采摘相当的果实品质，同时降低采摘成本，使种植者能够扩大生产规模。
author: Geert Hekkert
contentHtml: UK-based Fieldwork Robotics has secured additional investment from SEED Innovations, the investment company chaired by entrepreneur Jim Mellon, to accelerate the commercial deployment of its autonomous raspberry harvesting robot.
